appeal_letter:
Re: Appeal of Denial for Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP), dates of service 05/10/2026 to 06/10/2026

Dear Cigna Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for the Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) requested by my provider for the period of 05/10/2026 to 06/10/2026.

The denial letter states that, based on Cigna's Medical Necessity Guidelines and the Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG) for Behavioral Health, the clinical information did not indicate symptoms severe enough to require an intensive program multiple days per week, that I am medically stable and able to perform activities of daily living, and that I could be safely treated at a lower level of care such as weekly standard outpatient therapy.

I respectfully ask that this decision be reconsidered in light of the clinical circumstances. The clinical record reflects severe OCD with daily compulsions lasting six or more hours, which has contributed to job loss and an inability to leave the house. The determination that I can perform activities of daily living does not appear to account for this level of functional impairment. In addition, the record reflects that standard weekly outpatient therapy was already attempted for six months without improvement, which is the lower level of care identified in the denial. My provider has indicated that a stepped-up level of care in the form of IOP is appropriate given this history.

I ask that Cigna re-review the request using the complete clinical information, including the documented duration and severity of daily compulsions, the functional impact described above, and the prior six-month trial of standard outpatient therapy that did not produce improvement. I request that the denial be overturned and that the requested IOP be authorized for the dates of service noted above.

Please let me know if any additional documentation would assist in your review.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Sincerely,
[Member Name]
[Member ID]
[Contact Information]
missing_evidence_checklist: Provider letter of medical necessity detailing OCD severity, daily compulsion duration, and rationale for IOP over standard outpatient therapy
Documentation of the six-month trial of standard weekly outpatient therapy and the lack of improvement (treatment dates, clinician notes, outcome measures)
Standardized OCD severity assessment results if available (for example, a validated symptom rating scale)
Records documenting functional impairment, including job loss and inability to leave the house
Any treatment plan or guideline reference the provider relies on supporting step-up to IOP
Member identification number, plan information, and the denial reference number
